home.tagline: Start from a real workflow instead of digging through placeholders.
home.snapshotEmpty: Start the proxy to see live traffic

inspect.title: Inspect Sessions
inspect.subtitle: Open captured HTTP and HTTPS traffic

breakpoints.title: Debug Breakpoints
breakpoints.subtitle: Pause, inspect, and resume intercepted traffic

ai.title: AI Workspace
ai.subtitle: Attach sessions and analyze requests with AI

certificates.title: Install Certificates
certificates.subtitle: Prepare HTTPS MITM and trust setup

capture.title: Capture Requests
capture.description: Start a local HTTP proxy, then point your client or system proxy settings at it.
capture.configureClient: Configure the client or system HTTP proxy to {{address}}. New HTTP requests will appear in Sessions.

upstream.title: Upstream Proxy
upstream.description: Forward captured traffic through an upstream proxy (e.g. Clash at 127.0.0.1:7890).

sessions.noCapturesHint: Start the proxy and make HTTP requests to see them listed here.

breakpoint.title: Breakpoint
breakpoint.rulesDescription: Define rules to pause matching HTTP requests for inspection and editing.
breakpoint.noRulesHint: Add a rule to pause HTTP requests matching a host, path, or method for inspection and editing.
breakpoint.disabledWarning: Breakpoints are globally disabled — toggle the switch above to activate

script.title: Scripts
script.noScriptsHint: Create scripts to automatically modify HTTP requests and responses as they pass through the proxy.
script.name: Name
script.disabledWarning: Scripts are globally disabled — toggle the switch above to activate

tool.title: Tool Output
tool.empty: Tool invocation results will appear here.

skill.label: Skill
skill.noSkillsHint: Install skill packages to extend agent capabilities

composer.placeholder: Describe the HTTP issue you want to investigate

runtime.notConfigured: AI runtime not configured. Configure it in AI Configuration.

empty.prompt: What would you like to investigate today?
empty.hint: Ask me to analyze attached HTTP sessions, explain failures, compare traffic, or spot security and performance issues.

preset.securityRisk: Does this endpoint have security risks?
preset.redirectChain: Help me trace the redirect chain
preset.performanceSlow: Why is this request so slow?
preset.summarizeTraffic: Summarize the key issues in this traffic
preset.requestFailurePrompt: Help me analyze why this request or response is failing. Identify likely causes from the attached HTTP sessions and suggest the next debugging steps.
preset.securityRiskPrompt: Review the attached HTTP sessions for security risks. Check cookies, authentication flow, CORS, CSP, headers, and any exposed sensitive data.
preset.redirectChainPrompt: Help me trace the redirect or login flow in these attached sessions. Explain where the chain changes and what might be causing the problem.
preset.performanceSlowPrompt: Analyze why this request flow is slow. Look at timing, payload size, caching, compression, and any unnecessary round trips in the attached sessions.
preset.compareRequestsPrompt: Compare the attached HTTP requests and responses. Highlight the important differences and explain which one is likely causing the issue.
preset.summarizeTrafficPrompt: Summarize the attached HTTP traffic. Point out the key requests, suspicious behavior, failures, and the most important problems to investigate next.

history.title: Conversations
history.deleteDescription: This action cannot be undone. This conversation will be permanently deleted.
history.noConversationsHint: Start a conversation and it will be saved here

runtime.name: Name
runtime.maxContextTokensHint: Maximum context window size in tokens. 0 = auto-detect based on runtime type.

cert.title: HTTPS Certificate Setup
cert.description: To inspect HTTPS traffic, a CA certificate must be generated and trusted by the system. The proxy will use this CA to sign certificates for each domain, enabling decryption of HTTPS requests and responses.

overview.title: HTTPS Readiness
overview.description: Check whether certificate generation, system trust, and HTTPS interception are ready.

cards.authorityDescription: Generate the local root CA used to sign intercepted HTTPS traffic.
cards.trustPending: Generate a CA certificate before installing trust.

configuration.title: HTTPS Configuration
configuration.description: Generate the CA, trust it, then restart capture if needed.

notice.title: When this takes effect
notice.readyRunning: HTTPS certificates are ready. Restart capture to apply them to the current proxy session.
notice.readyStopped: HTTPS certificates are ready. They will be used the next time capture starts.
notice.notReady: Generate and trust the CA certificate before HTTPS traffic can be decrypted.

cert.generateHint: Generate a root CA certificate for signing HTTPS certificates.
cert.trustHint: Trust the CA in your system keychain.
cert.restartHint: Stop and restart the proxy capture to enable HTTPS interception.

cors.overrideDescription: Inject CORS headers into responses, allowing cross-origin requests from any origin. Useful for local development.

autoStartProxy.label: Auto-start Proxy
autoStartProxy.description: Automatically start the proxy when the app launches

maxSessions.label: Max Sessions
maxSessions.description: Maximum number of captured sessions to keep in memory (0 = unlimited)

proxy.title: Proxy Configuration
proxy.description: Configure the listening address and upstream proxy
proxy.runningWarning: Stop the proxy to modify these settings

runtimes.title: AI Configuration
runtimes.description: Configure the local and remote runtimes used by the AI workspace.

skills.title: AI Skills
skills.description: Manage installed skills available to the AI workspace.

update.clickToView: Click to view the release details
